statement: Every function on a finite field is given by a polynomial.
lean_statement:
theorem exists_polynomial_of_function_on_finite_field
    {K : Type*} [Field K] [Finite K] (f : K → K) :
    ∃ p : Polynomial K, ∀ x : K, p.eval x = f x
proof:
Proof (Lagrange interpolation): since `K` is finite, write its elements as a finite set. For each
`a : K`, define the Lagrange basis polynomial
`L_a(X) = ∏_{b ≠ a} (X - b) / (a - b)`, so that `L_a(a) = 1` and `L_a(b) = 0` for `b ≠ a`.
Then the polynomial `p(X) = ∑_{a : K} f(a) * L_a(X)` satisfies `p(a) = f(a)` for every `a`.
